project_scope_decision.meaning: Four healthy GPUs are sufficient for project scope. This does not waive the unexplained 8/7 mapping, admit either affected device, or authorize reset/reload/reboot actions.

gate.reason: Physical/driver, NVML, and PyTorch enumeration are inconsistent, and a visible device has active ECC, row-remap, and Xid failures.

scope.project_agent_role: preserve evidence and perform post-clearance read-only verification only

interpretation: Both devices have a present, enabled, full-width PCIe Gen4 link and are bound to the NVIDIA driver.
The missing runtime device fails NVIDIA RM initialization despite the live PCIe link and driver binding.
Zero endpoint AER totals do not negate GPU-internal ECC, Xid, or RM initialization failures.
The upstream root-port AER view, BMC telemetry, and vendor hardware diagnostics remain administrator tasks.

administrator_actions: Drain affected workloads and establish a maintenance window before state-changing diagnostics or recovery.
Prevent new allocations of unresolved devices through the scheduler or authoritative allocation process.
Preserve pre-change PCI mapping, ECC, row-remap, Xid, AER, BMC, and recovery-action evidence.
Record change owner, ticket/reference, maintenance timestamps, rollback plan, and final device disposition.
Stop repeated probing if a health query produces another Xid or device disappearance.
project_actions: Do not run CUDA, NCCL, DDP, memory, performance, or training workloads.
Do not use numeric GPU indices to mask the enumeration mismatch.
Wait for explicit administrator clearance before any further device query.

administrator_diagnostic_request.required_result: A preserved pre-change PCI/device-minor/UUID/NVML mapping and relevant kernel, AER, BMC, and platform health evidence.
A root-cause classification for repeated NVIDIA RmInitAdapter failure 0x62:0x40:1941 despite an enabled full-width PCIe link and driver binding, plus a restore-or-exclude disposition.
A vendor/runbook-backed disposition for uncorrectable ECC, pending row remap, Xid 95, and the driver Reset recommendation.
Selection of remediation path A (full 8/8/8) or path B (approved stable isolation with an exact PCI/UUID allowlist).
An administrator review of upstream root-port AER, BMC/platform telemetry, and vendor hardware diagnostics because endpoint AER counters are zero and root-port totals are not exposed at the endpoint.

A.name: restore_full_eight_gpu_inventory
required_outcomes: PCI, driver, NVML, and PyTorch enumerate the same eight expected devices.
PCI and UUID mappings are one-to-one with no silent index shift.
Every expected device has zero volatile uncorrectable ECC, no row-remap pending/failure, and no active recovery action.
Every device with nonzero aggregate uncorrectable ECC has an explicit administrator disposition before inclusion.
A recorded observation window has no new ECC, Xid, AER, or device disappearance.
A healthy four-GPU candidate set is identified by PCI and UUID.

minimum_observation.note: Use a longer interval when required by the hardware vendor or site runbook.

B.name: administrator_approved_stable_isolation
required_outcomes: The expected device count and exact PCI/UUID allowlist are documented.
Every excluded device has a reason, isolation mechanism, owner, and final disposition.
The scheduler or authoritative allocation layer enforces the exclusion; process-local numeric indexing is insufficient.
NVML and PyTorch enumerate exactly the approved allowlist with identical mappings.
The approved set contains a four-GPU candidate set meeting the same health criteria as path A.
The approval records scope, effective time, expiry/review conditions, and invalidation triggers.

post_remediation_revalidation.g0_g_pass_requires: All selected path outcomes and post-remediation checks pass with no unresolved health or mapping failure.
post_remediation_revalidation.downstream_evidence_invalidation: Kernel, driver, topology, UUID, or hardware changes make affected G2/G5/G6/G8 GPU evidence stale.
